name: ANCF Digital Projects
subtitle: 100 Antinatalism & Childfree Tools, Games, Guides, and Reflection Projects

projects.title: Antinatalism Intro Guide
projects.description: A calm, plain-language introduction to what antinatalism is — and is not. Now includes The Antinatalism Reader: a full 43-chapter companion book.

projects.title: Childfree Life Intro Guide
projects.description: An introduction to choosing a life without children — practically and emotionally. Now includes The Childfree Life: a full 44-chapter companion book.

projects.title: Pronatalism Pressure Detector
projects.description: A 360-degree interactive toolkit on pronatalism: understand its four levels and history, a message pressure-detector, a decoded field guide, a personal pressure profile, a coercion safety-check, a boundary composer, and rehearsal scenarios.

projects.title: Parenthood Assumption Quiz
projects.description: A 360-degree toolkit for examining the assumptions behind parenthood talk: an assumption decoder, an 18-entry library, a self-profile radar, a Socratic belief-examiner, root-tracing, a quiz, and reflection.

projects.title: “Is This My Choice?” Reflection Tool
projects.description: A decision-clarity studio for telling your own wishes from inherited pressure: a desire-vs-pressure map, a values clarifier, a whose-voice-is-it sorter, clarity signals, five classic perspective tools, and a private clarity report.

projects.title: Reproductive Autonomy Explainer
projects.description: A 360-degree explainer on reproductive autonomy: the conditions a free choice needs, how coercion runs in both directions, a short history of reproductive rights, a personal autonomy self-check, scenarios, and an autonomy-respecting response generator.

projects.title: Consent and Birth Philosophy Page
projects.description: An interactive look at the consent argument in birth ethics: build the argument premise by premise, explore three senses of consent, Shiffrin's gold-coins analogy, an objection explorer, and a position finder.

projects.title: Suffering Risk Thought Experiment
projects.description: A structured thought experiment about risk and bringing life into the world: a probabilistic life-outcome simulator with a veil-of-ignorance test, the decision frames philosophers use, thought-experiment cards, and a where's-your-line tool.

projects.title: Adoption vs Birth Ethics Explainer
projects.description: A balanced comparison of ethical considerations around adoption and biological birth: five paths to caring for children, the existing-need vs created-need contrast, an honest look at adoption, and a priorities mapper that ranks paths to your values.

projects.title: “No Means No” Family Boundary Tool
projects.description: A 360-degree boundary toolkit for repeated family questions: understand the FOG of pressure, generate calm boundary scripts, decode emotional blackmail, climb an escalation ladder, and rehearse holding the line.

projects.title: Childfree Budget Calculator
projects.description: A country-aware, editable model of funds a childfree budget may free up: pick your country for local currency and figures, with monthly category presets, a reallocation planner, and a compound 'freedom fund' projection.

projects.title: Parenthood Cost Estimator
projects.description: A neutral, country-aware estimator of the cost of raising a child across six phases: pick your country for local currency and figures, with a cumulative-cost chart by age and a phase breakdown. Estimates only.

projects.title: Time Freedom Calculator
projects.description: A 168-hour week allocator: see how your week divides, how much discretionary time remains, how caregiving changes it, and what your free hours add up to over years — with a week visualizer and lifetime projection.

projects.title: Sleep Loss Simulator
projects.description: An illustrative model of how a nightly sleep shortfall during early caregiving accumulates into a cumulative sleep debt, by phase, with a timeline chart and relatable equivalents. Not medical advice.

projects.title: Life Goals Priority Mapper
projects.description: Add the goals that matter to you, sort them across three horizons (Now/Soon/Someday) and an importance-vs-effort matrix, and get a clear suggestion of where to start. A planning tool; everything stays on your device.

projects.title: Personal Peace Checklist
projects.description: A gentle toolkit for protecting your peace around repeated questions: a before/during/after peace plan with progress, a kit of calm exit-lines, a guided box-breathing grounder, and a personal support anchor.

projects.title: Pressure From Parents Response Generator
projects.description: Draft a reply to parental pressure that honors their love while holding your line: a warmth-to-firmness dial generator, an 'acknowledge-then-hold' structure, and a mapper for the fear underneath their words.

projects.title: Marriage and Children Conversation Tool
projects.description: Map where you and your partner align on the children question across seven key topics, see an alignment radar and where you differ, follow a guided conversation agenda, and find honest help for a real mismatch.

projects.title: Childfree Couple Planning Board
projects.description: An editable vision board for a couple (or anyone) to name what they're building together across six areas of life, sparked by suggestions, with a shared-vision statement builder. Everything stays on your device.

projects.title: Retirement Without Children Planner
projects.description: Answer 'who will look after you?' with a plan: a country-aware compound savings projection with a target/gap check, plus a care-network builder for the support money can't buy. Pick your country for local currency and defaults.

projects.title: Antinatalist Quote Archive
projects.description: A searchable, filterable archive of original study-prompts that rephrase ideas from the antinatalist tradition for reflection. Filter by theme or thinker, draw one at random, save favourites, and copy any.

projects.title: Childfree Quote Archive
projects.description: A searchable archive of original affirmations and reflections on living childfree — for journaling and quiet encouragement. Filter by theme, draw one at random, save favourites, and build your own affirmation.

projects.title: Ethics Flashcards
projects.description: A study deck of 24 flip-cards for the key terms in birth ethics and reproductive autonomy: filter by category, mark what you know, track progress, and run a focused study session.

projects.title: Philosophy Timeline
projects.description: A simplified, filterable study timeline of ideas feeding antinatalist and childfree thought across philosophy, religion, feminism, ethics, and ecology. Filter by strand, search, and tap any entry for detail.

projects.title: Benatar Argument Explainer
projects.description: David Benatar's asymmetry argument for antinatalism, laid out fairly and interactively: the four-cell matrix you can explore, a premise tester you toggle, and the strongest objections with replies.

projects.title: Consent Argument Explainer
projects.description: The consent argument in birth ethics, built up interactively: an everyday analogy, an 'is this valid consent?' sorter that reveals what birth uniquely lacks, a premise tester, and the leading replies.

projects.title: Risk Argument Explainer
projects.description: The risk argument made concrete: what makes a risk hard to justify, an interactive comparator pitting an everyday risk against creating a life across six factors, the three decision lenses, and the leading replies.

projects.title: Environmental Argument Explainer
projects.description: The ecological reasoning some weigh in family decisions, stated fairly and with its limits clear: weigh the considerations, see what actually moves the needle (systems vs individual births), and meet the strongest objections.

projects.title: Feminist Childfree Explainer
projects.description: How feminist thought has defended a woman's right to decide whether and when to have children — including none: the threads of the argument, key voices and milestones, and an honest look at the debates within feminism.

projects.title: Economic Pressure Explainer
projects.description: How money, housing, and work push family decisions in both directions. Name the forces, map which weigh on you with a two-direction pressure meter, and see why these are structures, not personal failings.

projects.title: Comic Strip Prompt Generator
projects.description: Generate gentle, metaphor-based three-panel comic premises about pressure, autonomy, and boundaries — rendered as a storyboard you can sketch from. Pick a theme and tone, draw new ones, save favourites, and copy.

projects.title: Instagram Caption Generator
projects.description: Draft a thoughtful, respectful caption for a post about childfree life or reproductive autonomy: pick topic, tone, length, emoji and hashtags, generate variants, grab curated hashtag sets, and save favourites.

projects.title: Debate Reply Generator
projects.description: Draft a calm, non-hostile reply to a common claim about not having children — aimed at the idea, not the person. See the hidden assumption each claim rests on, pick a style, get variants, and learn the acknowledge-reframe-redirect structure.

projects.title: Family Function Survival Script
projects.description: Assemble a calm pocket script for a family gathering — opener, deflection, firm line, graceful exit — tuned to the event and your style. Plus a field guide to the lines you'll likely hear with ready responses, and a readiness checklist.

projects.title: “When Are You Having Kids?” Reply Tool
projects.description: The most common question, answered. Pick a vibe and who's asking, get a kind one-liner, cycle for more, and build your three go-to replies to keep ready. Every reply stays polite — a boundary, never an insult.

projects.title: WhatsApp Group Rules Generator
projects.description: Pick your group's focus and how strict you want moderation, toggle the rules you want, and generate a ready-to-pin rules set with a live preview — inclusive and safety-first by default. Edit and copy.

projects.title: Community Notice Generator
projects.description: Choose a notice type — event, schedule change, welcome, fundraiser, poll, or general — fill the details, and get a tidy, friendly message you can post. Six templates with dynamic fields and a live preview. Edit and copy.

projects.title: Admin Warning Message Generator
projects.description: Draft a private moderation warning that names the behaviour, not the person's worth, and offers a way back. Pick the issue and an escalation tier — gentle nudge, formal warning, or final notice — add specifics, and copy. Includes the escalation ladder and four principles.

projects.title: Respectful Debate Guide
projects.description: Principles for disagreeing well, a quick self-audit of your own debate habits with a personalised reading, and a phrase-swap library that turns heat into light — so you protect both the conversation and the relationship.

projects.title: Bad Argument Detector
projects.description: Paste any argument and spot the classic logical fallacies hiding inside — each flagged with the matched phrase and a plain explanation. Includes a filterable, searchable field guide of fifteen fallacies. A study aid for reasoning well, not a verdict on people.

projects.title: Pronatalist Phrase Translator
projects.description: Common pressure phrases carry a hidden assumption. This translator shows what a phrase literally says, what it really means, the assumption underneath, and a kind reply — by paste-and-match or by browsing a filterable library. For understanding, never for winning.

projects.title: Emotional Blackmail Detector
projects.description: Emotional blackmail uses fear, obligation, and guilt (FOG) as leverage. Paste a message to notice those patterns for your own clarity, each with a grounding reframe, plus a guide to the three levers and common tactics. Non-diagnostic: a flag is a prompt to reflect, not proof of abuse.

projects.title: Gaslighting Phrase Detector
projects.description: Gaslighting is a pattern of making someone doubt their own memory or perception. This tool flags phrases associated with it and pairs each with a reality anchor, plus a technique guide and five ways to stay grounded. Non-diagnostic: a single phrase isn't proof; pattern and context matter.

projects.title: Caste/Community Name Warning Tool
projects.description: When critiquing reproductive pressure, aim at systems and behaviours — never at castes, communities, religions, or ethnic groups. This tool screens a draft for group-targeting language, explains why it backfires, suggests a refocus, and shows before/after rewrites.

projects.title: Identity Pressure Reflection Page
projects.description: Belonging is precious — and sometimes it carries expectations about children. Rate how much child-related pressure each part of your identity (family role, gender, culture, faith, class, peers, inner critic) carries, see where it's loudest in a sorted picture, learn how each shapes expectations, and reflect.

projects.title: Social Shame Explainer
projects.description: Shame is one of society's quietest tools for keeping people in line. This explainer shows how it operates around reproductive choices, separates shame from healthy guilt with an interactive sorter, decodes common shaming lines, and offers four shame-resilience steps.

projects.title: Cultural Pressure Map
projects.description: Reproductive pressure rarely comes from one place. Rate the common external sources — family, in-laws, peers, work, faith community, media, policy, society — to see where it's strongest for you in a sorted map, with an overall load reading and targeted coping moves for your top sources.

projects.title: Wedding Pressure Dialogue Simulator
projects.description: Weddings are peak season for 'you're next!' This branching simulator lets you rehearse calm responses across a three-round conversation that adapts to your choices, tracks the balance of warmth and firmness, and ends with a debrief on how it landed. Replay to explore other paths.

projects.title: Elder Advice Response Tool
projects.description: Elders often advise from deep love and a different era. This tool drafts a reply that honours their care and wisdom while keeping your boundary — respect and self-respect together. Pick the relationship and the concern underneath, set the warmth, generate variants, and decode what they may really mean.

projects.title: Childfree FAQ
projects.description: Calm, respectful answers to the questions childfree people hear most — across identity, relationships, family pressure, the future, society, and meaning. Filter by topic, search, open any question, and copy an answer to reuse. 24 original Q&As; respects people who choose differently.

projects.title: Antinatalism FAQ
projects.description: Clear, respectful answers to frequent questions and misconceptions about antinatalism — basics, the main arguments, common myths (tagged), ethics, living with it, and criticisms. Filter by topic, search, open a question, copy any answer. Explains the position fairly and notes where people disagree.

projects.title: Beginner Study Plan
projects.description: A gentle four-week path through this collection — foundations, the arguments, society and autonomy, then living it. Pick a pace, tick items as you go, and watch per-week and overall progress fill in. Each step links straight to a project in the archive.

projects.title: 30-Day Reading Challenge
projects.description: One small reflective prompt a day for thirty days. See today's prompt, jot a private note, tick each day, and watch the grid fill in. Jump to any day; skip and return freely — no streak to break. Thirty original prompts; progress and notes save on this device.

projects.title: Study Circle Dashboard
projects.description: Organise a respectful reading group in one place: topic, schedule, roles, discussion questions (add your own or seed starters), and toggleable ground rules. A readiness meter tracks how complete it is, and you can export a tidy plan to share. Saves on this device.

projects.title: Philosophy Comparison Tool
projects.description: Pick any two of nine positions and compare them side by side across seven dimensions — core claim, view on procreation and suffering, meaning, key figures, strongest point, and main objection. A neutral map for understanding, with a glossary; it doesn't rank the positions.

projects.title: Stoicism and Antinatalism Notes
projects.description: Study notes putting Stoic ideas (dichotomy of control, amor fati, premeditatio malorum, oikeiosis, virtue, death) in conversation with questions about birth. The Stoics were not antinatalists — a clear myth-buster — and the notes map honestly where the two views converge and diverge.

projects.title: Buddhism and Antinatalism Notes
projects.description: Respectful study notes on Buddhist themes (Four Noble Truths, dukkha, tanha, samsara, the Middle Way, compassion) alongside questions about birth. Buddhism is not antinatalist — a clear myth-buster — with an honest converge/diverge map. Speaks about ideas, never for any practitioner.

projects.title: Periyarism and Childfree Reflection
projects.description: Periyar E. V. Ramasamy led the rationalist Self-Respect Movement in twentieth-century Tamil Nadu, championing reason, dignity, anti-caste justice, and women's autonomy. These notes connect his ideas to childfree reflection — accurately, framing him as a reformer (not an antinatalist) — with a bio, idea cards, and a resonance picker.

projects.title: Nietzsche Misuse Explainer
projects.description: Nietzsche is one of the most misquoted thinkers online. This explainer shows how the distortions happen, decodes six common misuses against closer readings with sources, and tests whether famous lines are genuine, distorted, or misattributed. He was life-affirming — not a patron saint for nihilism.

projects.title: Rationalism Toolkit
projects.description: Seven practical tools for thinking clearly about emotive topics — steelman, separate fact/value/definition, what-would-change-my-mind, seek disconfirmation, taboo the word, check the base rate, notice motivated reasoning. Learn each, run any on a claim you choose, and audit how often you use them.

projects.title: Mini Car Race Game: Escape Pronatalist Pressure
projects.description: Steer your car between three lanes and slip past the falling 'pressure' blocks — a light, playful metaphor for steering past pressure with a steady hand. Keyboard, on-screen buttons, or tap to play; the speed ramps up and your best score is saved.

projects.title: Pong Game: Choice vs Pressure
projects.description: Keep 'Choice' bouncing and don't let it drop. Each return scores and the ball speeds up — a light reminder that staying in the game takes a steady paddle and a calm eye. Keyboard, on-screen buttons, or drag to play; best rally saved.

projects.title: Platform Game: Boundary Runner
projects.description: Keep running and hop over each hurdle in this tiny endless runner — a light metaphor for clearing the small obstacles that come at you, one jump at a time. Space, tap, or click to jump; speed ramps up and your best is saved.

projects.title: Quiz Game: Myth vs Reality
projects.description: Myth or reality? Read each of 14 statements about antinatalism and childfree life and decide — then see the explanation. A friendly way to test and sharpen what you know, with a score, a streak, best-streak saved, and no trick questions.

projects.title: Memory Game: Ethical Concepts
projects.description: Flip the cards and match each key ethics term to its definition. Every match reveals the concept in a sentence — a playful way to make the vocabulary stick. Six term/definition pairs, move counter, and a best (fewest-moves) score saved.

projects.title: Decision Tree Game
projects.description: Walk a short, branching scenario about handling family pressure at the dinner table. Your choices change the path and earn 'freedom points' for calm, self-respecting moves, ending with a tailored debrief and your path. Replay to explore other routes; best run saved.

projects.title: “Auntie Uncle Chase” Arcade Game
projects.description: Scoot around collecting gold 'freedom tokens' while keeping your distance from the well-meaning 'pressure' chasers. A friendly metaphor — nobody's the villain — and a playful test of holding your own space. Arrows, WASD, or the on-screen pad; chasers speed up over time; best saved.

projects.title: Population Growth Visual Toy
projects.description: A simple toy for an idea that surprises almost everyone: how fast exponential growth compounds. Slide starting size, yearly growth rate, and years, watch the curve (with a lower-rate comparison line), and read the final size, doublings, and doubling time. Pure maths; implies nothing about anyone's choices.

projects.title: Resource Allocation Simulator
projects.description: A simple toy showing how a fixed pool (budget, free hours, or living space) divides per person as a household grows. Pick a resource, set the pool and the number of people, and see each share and a row of people. Just division — it implies nothing about ideal family size, and real households share in ways division ignores.

projects.title: Freedom Points Game
projects.description: Five everyday moments, one choice each — pick the response that protects your autonomy and stays kind. Each choice gives feedback on the 'freedom' and 'warmth' dials and points; the top answers keep both. A playful mirror and good practice. Best score saved; replay anytime.

projects.title: Personal Manifesto Builder
projects.description: Assemble a short, dignified personal manifesto about your choices.

projects.title: Childfree Bio Generator
projects.description: Generate a friendly profile bio that reflects a childfree identity.

projects.title: Antinatalist Statement Builder
projects.description: Draft a careful, non-hateful statement of an antinatalist view.

projects.title: Instagram Carousel Text Builder
projects.description: Generate slide-by-slide text for an educational carousel post.

projects.title: Protest Poster Text Generator
projects.description: Create short, positive slogans for awareness about reproductive autonomy.

projects.title: Debate Notes Organizer
projects.description: Organise a debate: your claims, their claims, evidence, and common ground.

projects.title: Book Summary Template Tool
projects.description: Generate a clean template to summarise any book you read on these topics.

projects.title: Video Script Generator
projects.description: Draft a short, respectful explainer-video script.

projects.title: Article Outline Generator
projects.description: Produce a balanced article outline on a chosen topic.

projects.title: Community Newsletter Template
projects.description: Generate a friendly newsletter skeleton for a community group.

projects.title: Legal Rights Awareness Page
projects.description: General awareness of rights related to reproductive autonomy — not legal advice.

projects.title: Workplace Childfree Bias Notes
projects.description: Notes on subtle workplace assumptions about parenthood — and staying professional.

projects.title: Healthcare Conversation Checklist
projects.description: Prepare for a respectful, productive talk with a healthcare provider.

projects.title: Sterilization Discussion Preparation Tool
projects.description: Draft respectful questions to discuss permanent contraception with a doctor.

projects.title: Couple Compatibility Questions
projects.description: Conversation-starter questions to explore alignment on children and life.

projects.title: Mental Health Boundary Notice
projects.description: Draft a caring notice that sets a topic boundary while signposting support.

projects.title: Crisis-Safe Community Policy Page
projects.description: A model policy for handling crisis moments safely and kindly in a community.

projects.title: Anti-Harassment Rules Page
projects.description: Clear anti-harassment rules that protect every member.

projects.title: Respectful Language Guide
projects.description: Word choices that keep advocacy firm yet kind.

projects.title: Safe Moderation Checklist
projects.description: A pre-flight checklist for moderators keeping a space safe and fair.

projects.title: Global Childfree Voices Map
projects.description: A conceptual map of how childfree conversations appear across regions.

projects.title: Tamil Childfree Voices Page
projects.description: A respectful page acknowledging Tamil-language voices on autonomy and childfree life.

projects.title: South Asian Pronatalism Explainer
projects.description: A careful explainer on pronatalist pressures in South Asian contexts — without stereotyping.

projects.title: Women and Reproductive Pressure Page
projects.description: How reproductive pressure specifically affects women — with full respect for mothers.

projects.title: Men and Provider Pressure Page
projects.description: How provider and 'legacy' pressures affect men — with empathy.

projects.title: LGBTQ+ Childfree Inclusion Page
projects.description: Centring LGBTQ+ experiences within childfree and autonomy conversations.

projects.title: Disabled People and Reproductive Autonomy Page
projects.description: Affirming the full reproductive autonomy of disabled people.

projects.title: Poverty and Parenthood Pressure Explainer
projects.description: How poverty shapes family pressures — critiquing systems, never poor people.

projects.title: Future Without Forced Parenthood Vision Board
projects.description: Imagine and sketch a future built on free, supported reproductive choice.

projects.title: ANCF Digital Projects Final Archive
projects.description: The closing archive: a thank-you and a map back into the whole collection.
